title: Savory Spiced Egyptian Chicken Kebabs
description: A cultured and snappy Egyptian dish perfect for a date night, featuring white wine vinegar and a blend of aromatic spices, this recipe is FODMAP-friendly and sure to impress.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together white wine vinegar, olive oil, ground cumin, smoked paprika, salt, black pepper, and garlic-free spice blend to make the marinade.
Add the chicken pieces to the marinade and toss to coat, then cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat, threading the marinated chicken pieces onto skewers if using wooden skewers, soak them in water for 30 minutes beforehand.

Remove the chicken from the marinade, letting any excess liquid drip off, and grill the chicken kebabs for 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally, until they reach an internal temperature of 165 degrees.
Transfer the kebabs to a serving platter and let them rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve the kebabs hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro if desired.
